I loved the music. The piano was my solace, my confidant, my voice in a world that often felt deaf to my cries. Each note I played carried the weight of my emotions, my fears, my hopes. In those moments of creation, I found a fleeting glimpse of transcendence, a brief respite from the relentless ache of existence. Music was my love, my curse, and my salvation.
Loved music, piano was my solace and confidant, voice in a world that often felt deaf to my cries.
